fields.description:
## Why
PLACEHOLDER epic to be designed via /joe-brainstorm. The workflow visual editor (epic
hump-seam-spice / story 744b) now does a faithful view+edit round-trip, but hands-on review
surfaced a set of larger architecture + UX items that are too big to build without proper
planning. This epic collects them so a future session can brainstorm + spec them. NONE of
the below is implemented yet unless noted; treat acceptance criteria as provisional.

## Context (the vision, from the session that created this)
The editor should let a human compose workflows by SELECTING prompts/scripts/MCP calls with
clear contracts, and by authoring new prompts in place. The unifying need: every workflow
step kind (scripted op, prompt/agent, MCP call) should advertise its INPUT contract, OUTPUT
contract, and a description, so the editor can present a typed palette and validate edits.

## Scope / brainstorm agenda
1. PROMPT ARCHITECTURE (the big one).
   - A prompt LIBRARY surfaced as a dropdown listing BOTH project prompts (.rebar/prompts/
     <id>.md) AND prompts shipped with rebar (built-in). Reviewers are a SUBSET of prompts,
     NOT a separate concept — "built-in prompt" must not mean "reviewer" (the old
     conflation; partially corrected in-session for the editor display + the workflow
     runtime, see below — but the full model is this epic).
   - Built-in prompts that are NOT reviewers (general workflow prompts). Need a packaged
     location + resolver precedence: project override > built-in > reviewer-catalog.
   - Create a new prompt IN THE UI → created in the library: when editing FROM the rebar
     repo itself, the new prompt is shipped-with-rebar (packaged); when editing from a
     CLIENT project, it is a project prompt (.rebar/prompts/). 
   - Prompt CATEGORIES by input/output contract (a scalable taxonomy, not building them all):
     review (read-only → findings JSON), completion-verifier (PASS/FAIL verdict — different
     output contract), exploration agent (find info on web/in codebase), analysis/transform
     (operate on input data), code-writing (modify project code). Core requirement: specify
     prompt TEXT + INPUT contract + OUTPUT contract per prompt.
   - Distinguish SINGLE-TURN prompts from AGENTIC prompts with tool use — two different
     concepts, used differently in workflows (e.g. single-turn for transform/classify;
     agentic for explore/verify). The IR + editor must represent the distinction.
2. SCRIPT OPERATIONS: surface input contract, output contract, and description for each
   scripted built-in (rebar.llm.workflow.steps), mirroring the prompt contracts, so the
   editor palette/inspector can show what a script step consumes/produces.
3. MCP CALLS as workflow steps: workflows need to include MCP tool calls as a first-class
   step kind (with their input/output contract surfaced like the above).
4. EDITABLE PROMPTS IN THE UI (was Q7): edit prompt text in the editor and WRITE it to the
   .md file on Save — a built-in edit becomes a PROJECT OVERRIDE (.rebar/prompts/<id>.md),
   never mutating the packaged file; PRESERVE front-matter; refuse/guard variant-overlay
   prompts to avoid corruption. MUST ship with round-trip tests proving no corruption.
5. IN-EDITOR JSON VALIDATION + HELP (was Q3): live validation of the Rebar config JSON field
   (red error before Save), and in-editor help walking the user through element types, what
   each does, and the expected JSON format per kind. (Save-time validation already exists.)
6. BRANCH / CONNECTION UX refinements (was Q2): we shipped condition labels + then/false
   default-flow slash + an "after" continuation label, deliberately avoiding synthetic
   gateways (round-trip-fragile). Revisit if a clearer representation is wanted (e.g.
   converging gateways, explicit parallel gateways) — weigh against round-trip fragility.
7. PROPERTIES PANEL: structured per-field entries (uses/prompt/mode/model/with/loop bounds)
   instead of the single Rebar-config JSON textarea, once contracts (1-3) exist to drive them.

## What was already done in-session (so don't redo)
- Editor view+edit round-trip works (744b CLOSED): vendored bpmn-js bundle (auto-layout
  replaced by a Python layered layout; properties panel; served locally, no CDN), faithful
  bpmn-moddle E2E + real-browser Playwright E2E tiers (tests/e2e/), branch '@'-id Save bug
  fixed, start/end events, connection labels, generic-task→scripted (no silent drop),
  loud-failure on unmappable elements, morph-preserves-config (verified).
- Editor's prompt DISPLAY (resolve_prompts in editor.py) shows resolved prompt text for both
  built-in reviewers and project prompts (read-only) — a small step toward item 1; the full
  library/dropdown/create-in-UI is THIS epic.
- The PydanticAI cutover (story d6d1, separate from this epic) is largely done: pydantic_ai
  is the default runner, validated live across all ops; the langchain/langgraph/deepagents
  code was removed. REMAINING for d6d1 (NOT this epic): remove the deps from pyproject
  [agents]; re-home tracing to a [tracing] OTel→Langfuse exporter (wired/unverified); update
  docs/llm-framework.md + fix the get_prompt-vs-git-canonical drift.

## Relevant code
- IR + schema: src/rebar/schemas/workflow.v2.schema.json; rebar/llm/workflow/{schema,lint,lint_refs,migrate}.py
- Steps registry (scripted): rebar/llm/workflow/steps.py
- Editor: rebar/llm/workflow/editor.py + editor_assets/ (bundle) ; serializer rebar/llm/workflow/bpmn.py
- Prompts: rebar/llm/prompts.py (reviewer catalog reviewers/catalog.json + reviewers/*.md;
  project .rebar/prompts/*.md). Runtime prompt resolution: rebar/llm/workflow/runs.py
  (RunnerAgentStep) — currently reviewer-centric; generalize per item 1.
- Runner/contracts: rebar/llm/runner.py (pydantic_ai), contracts.py, structured.py, pai_tools.py

## Acceptance Criteria (PROVISIONAL — refine in brainstorm)
- [ ] A unified prompt model where reviewers are a subset of prompts; built-in non-reviewer
      prompts are first-class and resolvable (project > built-in > reviewer).
- [ ] Prompts + script ops + MCP calls each declare input contract, output contract, and a
      description, consumed by the editor palette/inspector.
- [ ] Single-turn vs agentic prompt distinction represented in the IR + editor.
- [ ] Editor: select a prompt from a library dropdown (project + built-in); author a new
      prompt in the UI saved to the correct location (built-in when editing rebar itself,
      project otherwise); edit prompt text with safe write-back + round-trip tests.
- [ ] MCP calls usable as workflow steps.
- [ ] In-editor JSON validation + element/contract help.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] The brainstorm produces a spec + child stories for the above, with clear contracts and
      a migration path that preserves the v2 IR round-trip.

## References
- Session log: search "WORKFLOW EDITOR + PYDANTIC CUTOVER" (this session's log).
- Related: story roomy-spine-mirth (editor UX remainder — to be parented here), epic
  hump-seam-spice (workflow-engine-v2), story d6d1/worn-mire-lath (the cutover).
